title: Golden Globes 2026: Best Supporting Actor Predictions
text: The webpage provides prediction scores for the 2026 Golden Globes nominations across film and television categories, with focus on the Best Supporting Actor category. The article highlights overall prediction accuracy across multiple categories. For the film categories specifically, Gold Derby user Luketheu achieved the highest score by correctly predicting 91% of nominations, securing 71 of 78 slots accurately. Among editors, Marcus James Dixon led with 85.9% accuracy, while expert Cole Jaeger topped the professional predictions at 89.7%. Regarding the supporting actor race, the article notes that experts and users successfully predicted several high-profile nominations, with strategic use of two Super Bets (500 points each) contributing to winning scores. The data indicates that nearly 3,000 people participated in film predictions across 13 categories. The close competition among predictors with Luketheu tying with 13 others before winning on points suggests the supporting actor category presented moderate difficulty for forecasting. The ceremony, hosted by Nikki Glaser, aired January 11 on CBS and Paramount+.
